title: Strand loopback bend and broken grouped strand spacing
summary: Four visible fiber strands overshoot their intended bend point and loop back instead of making a simple orthogonal 90-degree bend. The Yellow/Violet routed pair also fails to maintain grouped strand spacing on the vertical run, while the Slate/White pair preserves spacing apart from the shared bend defect.
visually_observable_issues: Slate/White and Yellow/Violet strand routes pass beyond the intended bend location, then return back on themselves to complete the turn.
The bends do not read as clean simple 90-degree orthogonal route transitions.
The Slate/White pair maintains a consistent spacing gap across the route, but still has the loopback bend defect.
The Yellow/Violet pair spreads too far apart on the vertical run and does not preserve the grouped strand spacing requirement.
The grouped Yellow/Violet route no longer stays visually nested in proper adjacent fiber color order through the vertical segment.

broken_requirements: Splice paths must stay within the max two-corner bend budget.
Bends should be simple orthogonal route transitions, not overshoot-and-return loopback shapes.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: All four visible strands route past the intended bend point and loop back, creating an avoidable extra bend/loopback shape instead of a clean 90-degree turn.

broken_requirements: Grouped strands should preserve hierarchy-aware routing and lane bands where possible.
Strands from the same routing group should stay adjacent or nearby through route transitions.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The Yellow/Violet pair does not remain nested as a coherent grouped route on the vertical run.

likely_broken_rules.rule_name: Fiber Strand Overlap, Crossing, and Collision
broken_requirements: Center vertical splice lanes must meet minimum spacing without unrelated lane drift.
Routes must remain visually traceable and must not use poor lane geometry when a cleaner path is available.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The Yellow/Violet vertical spacing is inconsistent with the expected paired lane spacing, and the loopback bend makes all four routes harder to trace.

broken_requirements: Individual fiber strand routes must maintain the 24px grid pitch in all directions.
Spacing applies around bends, lane transitions, fan-out exits, fusion splice dots, and between strand groups.
Bends that are too close together or visually confusing fail readability.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The Yellow/Violet vertical run does not preserve the required grouped strand spacing, and the bend shape creates confusing route geometry.

broken_requirements: The canvas grid must use a 24px base pitch on both axes.
Grid lines, lane reservation, and strand spacing must not stretch or compress the pitch to fill space.
Strands inside the same buffer tube should use adjacent or nearby lanes.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The Yellow/Violet spacing appears stretched on the vertical run instead of preserving the expected adjacent grid pitch.

broken_requirements: Fiber strands inside a group must preserve the standard visual color order.
Grouped strands should remain ordered while routing and bending.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The Yellow/Violet pair visually separates during the vertical route, undermining the expected adjacent ordered group behavior.

partially_followed_rules.why_it_partially_passes: The Slate/White pair maintains a consistent spacing gap across the route, although it still fails because of the loopback bend shape.

notes: This should be treated as a hard bad example because the defects are structural routing failures, not only aesthetic issues.
Expected correction: each strand should make a clean orthogonal 90-degree bend at the intended grid point without overshooting and returning.
Expected correction: strands routed as a color-ordered group should stay adjacent through horizontal runs, vertical runs, and bends while preserving the 24px routing/grid pitch.
Slate/White is useful as a contrast case inside the same image: its spacing behavior is acceptable, but its bend geometry is still bad.
